[CRC 2.115.]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE BARBARA SHANLEY TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Petition For Order Accepting Resignation Of Trustee And For Appointment Of Successor Co-Trustees (Original Petition Filed In Consolidated Case Ptr-22-305373 On May-25-2022. Amended Petition Filed On Nov-02-2022)
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing per modified order after the judicial officer's review: 1. USAA Federal Savings Bank's tender of resignation is accepted as Trustee of the BARBARA SHANLEY 1996 IRREVOCABLE TRUST, effective immediately upon filing of a receipt of the transfer of the assets of such trust, other than reasonable reserve, to Jacquelynne Ocaña and Catherin Ocaña, as successor co-trustees. 2. Jacquelynne Ocaña and Catherine Ocaña are hereby appointed Successor Co-Trustees of the Barbara Shanley 1996 Irrevocable Trust to serve without bond. 3. Jacquelynne Ocaña and Catherine Ocaña as successor Co-Trustees, shall have the authority to act alone without the consent or concurrence of the other successor Co-Trustee with respect to any trust asset or matter. 4. In the event either Jacquelynne Ocaña or Catherine Ocaña is unable or unwilling to act as successor Co-trustee, then the other shall have the authority to act as sole trustee.

A courtesy copy of the ex parte petition to appoint the guardian ad litem and accompanying proposed order do not appear to have been sent to Room 202 for the file clerk to log in so that the ex parte petition could be assigned to an examiner. There were no exhibits attached to the supplement filed on 1/23/23. Trusts require 30 days' notice. Deadline for providing courtesy copies of any new filings is 3/22/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF AMALIA PULE
rulings.Calendar Matter: FUTURE ACCOUNTING
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MICHAEL LARRY MCLEAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/3/23. (No earlier availability.) Still no response to the issues posted for the 11/28/22 hearing. (1) No proof of service of the Notice of Petition to Administer Estate mailed at least 15 days before the hearing. (2) No proof of publication of the Notice of Petition to Administer Estate. (3) No supplement to the petition identifying the parents and setting forth their dates of death. (4) No waivers of bond. (If the estate has over $314,000, there should be attorneys willing to take on the estate case, as an attorney would be entitled to statutory fees. The petitioner does not personally pay legal fees for a decedent's estate.) New deadline to get the courtesy copies of any responsive pleadings to the assigned examiner is 3/23/23. If nothing is filed in time to get courtesy copies to the examiner by 3/23/23, and the petitioner does not contact the examiner to request more time, an appearance will likely be required on 4/3/23 for the Court to determine if the matter should be taken off calendar. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE TONKIN GRANDCHILDRENS TRUST OF 1999 FBO ASHTON TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To A)Establish Existence, Terms, And Validity Of Trust; B)Confirm Assets Of The Trust; And C) Appoint Successor Trustee
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/3/23. (No earlier date available.) No proposed order has been submitted or received yet by the assigned examiner as of 2/1/23. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] Deadline was 1/25/23. The petition must be supplemented with the beneficiary's address. [CRC 7.902.] Notice must be mailed directly to the person entitled to notice and also to an attorney representing the person entitled to notice, but not only to the attorney. [Probate Code Section 1214 and CRC 7.51(a) and (b).] Notice must include the language for remote appearance procedures, as required by LRSF 14.1.D.6. (A copy of the petition does not need to be attached to the Notice of Hearing/Proof of Service; it is sufficient to allege that a copy of the petition was mailed with the notice.) New deadline for submitting/filing responsive papers (verified supplement to the petition and proof of service of the complete Notice of Hearing with instructions regarding remote appearance) and providing the examiner with the proposed order and courtesy copies of those responsive pleadings is 3/22/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF THOMAS JOSEPH WALSH
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/3/23. (No earlier date available.) Notice must include the language for remote appearance procedures, as required by LRSF 14.1.D.6. Under what authority can the Court find that property held in joint tenancy, which passes to the surviving joint tenant by operation of law, is in fact community property? Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned examiner by 3/22/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JAMES T. PORRAS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/3/23. (No earlier availability.) Still no proposed order (or courtesy copies) received by the deadline of 1/25/23. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] Mailed notice must include the language for remote appearance procedures, as required by LRSF 14.1.D.6. The petitioner (the person named in item 2 of the petition) appears to be missing from item 8 of the petition. When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor through whom they take (or would have taken) shall be named and his or her date of death and relationship to the decedent shall be stated. [LRSF 14.18 C.] When did the decedent's brother die? New deadline for providing the examiner with the proposed order and courtesy copies is 3/23/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.
